decision_point: In an infant with developmental dysplasia of the hip failing bracing, is closed reduction or open surgical reduction preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an infant with developmental dysplasia of the hip failing bracing, is closed reduction or open surgical reduction preferred?
Options:
  1. Closed manual reduction
  2. Open surgical reduction
label: patient_dependent
label_rationale: Closed reduction under anesthesia is less invasive but has higher failure and osteonecrosis rates in select patterns; open reduction provides definitive clearance but carries surgical morbidity.

reviews.notes: which-option label stratum via llm_haiku — DDH management after failed bracing involves genuine trade-offs between invasiveness and durability—closed reduction risks higher osteonecrosis and redislocation in certain patterns (e.g., older age, high dislocation), while open reduction ensures anatomic reduction but adds surgical morbidity; the literature shows outcome variation by patient factors rather than consensus superiority of one approach.

auditor reasoning: Age, femoral head position, and intra-articular obstacles (limbus/pulvinar) directly gate closed vs open reduction. | Claude second opinion: Agree. Age and imaging findings (limbus, pulvinar interposition) are the actual decision drivers in standard teaching.
